title: Create M6 execution packets and baseline audit
summary: Instantiate the reviewed M6 Web / Windows Product Gate DAG as concrete task JSON, record the M6 startup audit from current main, and keep all product implementation unstarted until normal taskctl promotion after WM-0085 review.
acceptance: Creates concrete M6 task JSON packets from the reviewed DAG in coordination/reports/WM-0084-m6-task-dag.md

Records the startup audit confirming current roadmap authority, M5 closeout evidence, WM-0084 reviewed status, main/origin sync, clean worktree, inbox 0, no WM-0085+ preexisting tasks and no M6 implementation started before this task
Defines M6 as Web / Windows Product Gate, M7 as Early Access / public playtest preparation and M8 as 1.0 in task packet metadata where relevant

Downstream M6 task packets include milestone, ownerRole, reviewerRole, dependencies, allowed paths, forbidden paths, acceptance, validation commands, risks/file ownership, owner gates, M0-M5 regression coverage, benchmark impact, rollback model and Spark eligibility
Downstream M6 task packets remain proposed until normal taskctl promotion after WM-0085 is independently reviewed, integrated and marked done
No product runtime, simulation, content data, package manifest, lockfile, test behavior, benchmark artifact, M7 task or M7 implementation is changed

history.details: Created from the reviewed M6 entry prompt and WM-0084 M6 DAG after owner started the M6 Web / Windows Product Gate goal.
WM-0084 is done and reviewed; WM-0085 is the first non-implementation M6 execution-packet task.

Instantiated the reviewed M6 Web / Windows Product Gate DAG as WM-0086 through WM-0097 task JSON, recorded startup audit evidence, kept all downstream M6 tasks proposed, and started no product implementation or M7 work; required gates passed.

verified: No blocking findings. Verified WM-0086-WM-0097 preserve reviewed M6 DAG/current roadmap scope, downstream packets are proposed/unclaimed, no product or M7 work changed, and required review commands passed.

Reviewer verified WM-0085 with no findings. Integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality with pnpm 11.8.0. M6 task packets WM-0086 through WM-0097 are instantiated; no product implementation or M7 work started.

objective: Create the formal M6 task packets and startup audit from the reviewed M6 Web / Windows Product Gate DAG without starting implementation.
forbiddenPaths: downstream M6 implementation task claim before WM-0085 done
M7 task creation, promotion, claim, implementation or review
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: old M6/M7/M8/M9 inference leaks back into task packets; owner must cite current roadmap authority and WM-0084 reviewed DAG.
Risk: downstream packets are too broad, too narrow or miss owner gates; reviewer must compare against coordination/reports/WM-0084-m6-task-dag.md.
Risk: taskctl auto-promotes executable tasks after WM-0085 is done; this is allowed only after WM-0085 independent review and integration because the owner has explicitly started M6.
File ownership is limited to M6 coordination task packets, WM-0085 reports and narrow roadmap/project-state markers if needed.
benchmarkImpact: No product benchmark baseline or artifact changes. WM-0085 records M6 benchmark requirements only and preserves the existing 10 percent warning / 20 percent blocking thresholds plus M5 final hashes.
reviewRouting: Project-director completes to reviewer. Reviewer must verify that WM-0086 through WM-0097 exactly preserve the reviewed M6 Web / Windows Product Gate scope, dependencies and non-goals before WM-0085 can be integrated.
rollbackModel: Revert WM-0085 task/report files and the newly created WM-0086 through WM-0097 task JSON packets; leave WM-0084 reviewed prompt/DAG intact and M6 unstarted beyond owner authorization.
ownerGate: Block if any packet defines M6 as non-product-gate work
Block if any packet starts product implementation inside WM-0085
Block if task schema cannot represent the reviewed DAG without losing required owner gates
Block if M5 hashes or benchmark thresholds would be weakened
Do not create, promote, claim or implement M7

sparkEligibility.reason: This is milestone execution planning and task DAG instantiation under project-director ownership; Spark cannot own planning, product gates or final review.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Verified WM-0086-WM-0097 preserve reviewed M6 DAG/current roadmap scope, downstream packets are proposed/unclaimed, no product or M7 work changed, and required review commands passed.

integration.summary: Reviewer verified WM-0085 with no findings. Integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality with pnpm 11.8.0. M6 task packets WM-0086 through WM-0097 are instantiated; no product implementation or M7 work started.
